source_evidence: Appendix B.4, Eq. (24)-(27): d=Q-P, v=C-P; criteria: (24) P inside sphere v.v<=r^2; (25) Q inside (v-d).(v-d)<=r^2; closest point w=(v.d/d.d)d; (27) inside sphere (v - (v.d/d.d)d).(v - (v.d/d.d)d) <= r^2 with 0 <= w.d <= d.d.
purpose: Tests whether a truss member (segment) intersects a sphere restriction primitive by checking if either endpoint is inside, or the closest point on the segment to the sphere center is inside.
assumptions: Segment parameterized as X = P + td, 0 <= t <= 1
Collision if any of three criteria met: P inside, Q inside, or closest point between P and Q and inside
formula_or_algorithm: d = Q - P, v = C - P. Criterion 1: v.v <= r^2 (P inside). Criterion 2: (v-d).(v-d) = v.v - 2 v.d + d.d <= r^2 (Q inside). Criterion 3: closest point w = (v.d / d.d) d; it lies inside the sphere if (v - w).(v - w) <= r^2, and lies within the segment iff 0 <= w.d <= d.d (equivalently 0 <= t <= 1). Collision if any criterion holds.
validation_opportunities: unit tests: endpoint inside, tangent, chord through sphere, segment entirely outside

reuse_risk.explanation: Standard segment-sphere test with explicit closed-form conditions; robust and cheap.
